---
title: "3. Client Setup"
description-meta: "Install the openme CLI, add your profile config, and send your first knock."
keywords: [openme client setup, openme knock, openme CLI install, openme config]
---

This step covers the CLI client, which works on Linux, macOS and Windows.
For native apps see [Clients →](../clients/index.qmd).

---

## Install the CLI {#install}

#### Linux — Debian / Ubuntu package

{{< include _install-deb.qmd >}}

#### Linux — pre-built binary

{{< include _install-binary.qmd >}}

#### macOS

```bash
curl -Lo openme https://github.com/merlos/openme/releases/download/{{< var cli_tag_prefix >}}{{< var cli_version >}}/openme-darwin-arm64
chmod +x openme
sudo mv openme /usr/local/bin/
```

#### Windows

Download [`openme-windows-amd64.exe`](https://github.com/merlos/openme/releases/download/{{< var cli_tag_prefix >}}{{< var cli_version >}}/openme-windows-amd64.exe)
and add it to your `PATH`.

#### From source

```bash
go install github.com/merlos/openme/cli/cmd/openme@latest
```

---

## Add Your Profile Config {#config}

`openme add` on the server prints a ready-to-use YAML block. Copy it to
`~/.openme/config.yaml` on your client machine:

```yaml
profiles:
  default:
    server_host: "myserver.example.com"
    server_udp_port: 54154
    server_pubkey: "base64..."   # server's Curve25519 public key
    private_key:   "base64..."   # your Ed25519 private key
    public_key:    "base64..."   # your Ed25519 public key
    post_knock:    "ssh user@myserver.example.com"  # optional
```

The profile named `default` is used when no profile name is given.
See [Client Configuration reference](../configuration/client.qmd) for all fields.

---

## Knock {#knock}

```bash
# Verify the server is reachable (requires a prior valid knock)
openme status

# Send a knock using the default profile
openme knock

# Knock then immediately check health — full end-to-end test
openme status --knock
```

If `post_knock` is set, it runs automatically after a successful knock:

```bash
openme knock   # opens SSH, then connects automatically
```

Named profiles and advanced usage:

```bash
openme knock home               # named profile
openme knock --ip 10.0.0.5      # open firewall for a specific IP
openme knock home && ssh user@home.example.com  # inline without post_knock
```

---

## Further Reading

| Topic | Link |
|-------|------|
| All CLI flags | [CLI reference](../configuration/client.qmd) |
| Native apps (iOS, Android, macOS, Windows) | [Clients](../clients/index.qmd) |
| Firewall hardening | [Server → Firewall](../server/firewall.qmd) |
| Server configuration | [Server → Configuration](../server/configuration.qmd) |
